metadata.description: Short workload name, used to derive resource names.
Deployment region.
Random suffix to keep globally-unique names unique.
Entra ID tenant id that owns the Bot app registration.
App (client) ID of the Entra ID app registration backing the Bot. Must be SINGLE TENANT.
Publisher email for APIM.
Publisher name for APIM.
Foundry / AI Services account name (must be globally unique).
Foundry project name (logical sub-resource within the account).
Admin username for the jumpbox VM.
Admin password for the jumpbox VM. 12+ chars, complexity required.
Region.
Workload short name.
VNet address space.
APIM subnet prefix (External VNet mode requires a dedicated subnet).
Private Endpoint subnet prefix.
Azure Bastion subnet prefix. MUST be /26 or larger and named AzureBastionSubnet.
Jumpbox VM subnet prefix.
Region.
Foundry / AI Services account name.
Foundry project name.
SKU for the AI Services account.
Model deployment name (referenced by the agent).
Underlying model name to deploy.
Model version. Use a version GA in your region.
Tokens-per-minute capacity (thousands). 10 = 10K TPM.
Region.
Workload short name.
Foundry / AI Services account resource id.
Subnet id where the PE NIC will be attached.
Private DNS zone ids to bind to the PE (openai, cognitiveservices, services.ai.azure.com).
Region.
Workload short name.
Suffix to keep the APIM name globally unique.
Resource id of the subnet APIM will be injected into.
Publisher email shown in the developer portal.
Publisher name shown in the developer portal.
App (client) ID of the Bot — used as the JWT audience claim.
Entra ID tenant id.
Backend Foundry endpoint, e.g. https://aoai-xxx.openai.azure.com
SKU. Developer = non-prod, Premium = prod (only Premium supports VNet injection in production).
SKU capacity (units).

variables.$fxv#0:
<!--
  Global APIM policy
  ==================
  Applied to EVERY inbound request on this APIM instance.

  Security goals:
    1. Force HTTPS (enforced at the listener too, but defense-in-depth).
    2. Validate that the caller is the Microsoft Bot Framework Channel Service
       by checking the JWT signature against the public OpenID configuration
       at https://login.botframework.com/v1/.well-known/openidconfiguration
       and the issuer https://api.botframework.com.
    3. Validate the audience claim equals OUR bot's Microsoft App ID (the
       {{bot-app-id}} named value), so a token issued to a different bot
       cannot be replayed against us.
    4. Reject everything else with 401.

  Notes:
    - The bot-app-id named value is provisioned by infra/modules/apim.bicep.
    - require-scheme is case-sensitive on the header NAME but not VALUE.
    - We do NOT trust the X-Forwarded-For or Host headers — APIM strips/normalizes
      them; backend authorization is done with APIM's MSI, not the caller token.
-->
<policies>
  <inbound>
    <base />

    <!-- 1. Defense-in-depth: only HTTPS. -->
    <choose>
      <when condition="@(context.Request.OriginalUrl.Scheme != "https")">
        <return-response>
          <set-status code="403" reason="HTTPS required" />
        </return-response>
      </when>
    </choose>

    <!-- 2 + 3. Validate the Bot Framework JWT. -->
    <validate-jwt
        header-name="Authorization"
        failed-validation-httpcode="401"
        failed-validation-error-message="Unauthorized. Valid Bot Framework token required."
        require-scheme="Bearer"
        require-signed-tokens="true"
        require-expiration-time="true"
        clock-skew="300">
      <openid-config url="https://login.botframework.com/v1/.well-known/openidconfiguration" />
      <issuers>
        <issuer>https://api.botframework.com</issuer>
      </issuers>
      <audiences>
        <!-- Must equal the Bot's Microsoft App ID. Replay protection. -->
        <audience>{{bot-app-id}}</audience>
      </audiences>
      <required-claims>
        <claim name="aud" match="all">
          <value>{{bot-app-id}}</value>
        </claim>
      </required-claims>
    </validate-jwt>

    <!-- 4. Strip the inbound bearer so it never leaks to the backend. -->
    <set-header name="Authorization" exists-action="delete" />
  </inbound>

  <backend>
    <base />
  </backend>

  <outbound>
    <base />
    <!-- Remove server fingerprinting. -->
    <set-header name="Server"            exists-action="delete" />
    <set-header name="X-Powered-By"      exists-action="delete" />
    <set-header name="X-AspNet-Version"  exists-action="delete" />
  </outbound>

  <on-error>
    <base />
  </on-error>
</policies>
variables.$fxv#1:
<!--
  Per-API policy: bot-agent
  =========================
  Applied to the "bot-agent" API. Runs AFTER the global validate-jwt has accepted
  the request. Responsibilities:

    1. Rewrite the inbound /bot/api/messages call into the Foundry agent
       inference URL (kept here as a single rewrite-uri so you can swap in
       a richer transform later — e.g. extract the user utterance from the
       Bot Framework Activity and POST it to the agent's /chat/completions
       endpoint).

    2. Acquire an AAD access token for Cognitive Services using APIM's
       system-assigned managed identity (resource =
       https://cognitiveservices.azure.com/) and forward it as the Bearer
       to the backend. NO API KEYS in policy or config.

    3. Use the named backend "foundry-private", whose URL is the private
       Foundry endpoint. Because the VNet has Private DNS linked, the
       hostname resolves to the Private Endpoint IP — traffic never leaves
       the VNet.

    4. Add a correlation id for traceability.
-->
<policies>
  <inbound>
    <base />

    <!-- Tag every request for cross-service correlation in App Insights. -->
    <set-variable name="correlationId" value="@(Guid.NewGuid().ToString())" />
    <set-header name="x-correlation-id" exists-action="override">
      <value>@((string)context.Variables["correlationId"])</value>
    </set-header>

    <!-- Route to the private Foundry backend (Private Endpoint via Private DNS). -->
    <set-backend-service backend-id="foundry-private" />

    <!--
      Acquire an AAD token using APIM's MSI and inject it as the backend
      Authorization header. Resource = cognitiveservices.azure.com.
      RBAC: APIM's MSI must have the "Azure AI User" role on the Foundry
      account (granted in infra/modules/rbac.bicep).
    -->
    <authentication-managed-identity
        resource="https://cognitiveservices.azure.com/"
        output-token-variable-name="aadToken" />
    <set-header name="Authorization" exists-action="override">
      <value>@("Bearer " + (string)context.Variables["aadToken"])</value>
    </set-header>

    <!--
      Rewrite the path. The Bot Framework will POST /api/messages here;
      forward it to the project's agent endpoint. Adjust to your specific
      agent route (e.g. /openai/threads/runs or the agents preview API).
    -->
    <rewrite-uri template="/api/projects/sbodhankar-aveva-demo/agents/messages" />
  </inbound>

  <backend>
    <!-- Short, explicit timeout — fail fast instead of holding sockets. -->
    <forward-request timeout="60" buffer-request-body="true" />
  </backend>

  <outbound>
    <base />
    <set-header name="x-correlation-id" exists-action="override">
      <value>@((string)context.Variables["correlationId"])</value>
    </set-header>
  </outbound>

  <on-error>
    <base />
    <set-header name="x-correlation-id" exists-action="override">
      <value>@((string)context.Variables["correlationId"])</value>
    </set-header>
  </on-error>
</policies>

metadata.description: Bot Service is a global resource.
Workload short name.
Suffix to keep the name unique.
Entra ID tenant id (single-tenant bot).
App (client) ID of the Entra ID app registration backing the Bot.
Messaging endpoint — must be the APIM URL, not the AI service.
Existing Foundry / AI Services account name (parent scope).
Principal id of APIMs system-assigned managed identity.
Region.
Workload short name.
Suffix for global-uniqueness.
Bastion subnet resource id (must be AzureBastionSubnet).

VM admin username.

VM size.
Existing Foundry / AI Services account name (parent scope).
Principal id of APIMs system-assigned managed identity.
